Click Open to activate the open state of the accordion. After that, you can adjust the styles of the opened Accordion Item by going to the Design tab.
You can also manage the styles of the item in different states (default, hover, active, focus — in both closed and open states) on the Design tab, by adding and toggling them in the States list under the Style Sources section. Learn more
DE_HELP_COLLECTION_LIST_RIGHT_SETTINGS_C: CMS or databases help manage similar content on a website. With them, you can easily create sites with a large number of similar pages. Learn more
DE_HELP_COLLECTION_LIST_RIGHT_SETTINGS_F: Add a filter to control which collection items are displayed. Learn more
DE_HELP_COLLECTION_LIST_RIGHT_SETTINGS_P: Pagination is used to split large amounts of content into multiple pages, improving user experience, speeding up load times, and optimizing navigation. Learn more
DE_HELP_COLLECTION_LIST_RIGHT_SETTINGS_S:
When this option is enabled, each pagination page will have its own canonical URL.
If the option is disabled, pagination pages will point to the first collection page as the canonical URL.
Sort the collection items to change the order in which they are displayed on the page. By default, items are displayed by creation date, from newest to oldest. Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_ACCORDION:
Accordion is a widget with a list of items, each of which can be expanded or collapsed. It is used to group content. Unlike Tabs, Accordion allows you to expand multiple items at a time.

Each Accordion Item consists of:
  • Accordion Title
  • Accordion Content
You can add any items and widgets to an Item Title and Item Content.

On the Settings tab, in the right panel, you can add, delete, and reorder Accordion’s elements. Settings are also used to select the desired item to edit its content on the Canvas.

By default, all Accordion Items have the same structure and styles. You can use the Apply independent structure setting with the Accordion. It allows you to make the structure of Accordion Items independent of each other. That is, each item can have different elements and widgets with different styles. Once this setting is enabled, it cannot be cancelled.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_BADGE:
Taptop Badge is a branded widget featuring the Taptop logo and a link to the Taptop website. It is automatically added to all sites, except those using the annual Business Plan. By default, the widget is placed in the bottom right corner of the page.
You can customize the appearance of the widget in the Settings: choose the placement type (in the corner or in the footer), its position on the screen, and the color theme (light or dark).The Taptop Badge cannot be copied, moved, styled, or embedded into other widgets.The widget can only be removed by purchasing the annual Business Plan.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_BLOCK_LIST:
Block List is a List-like widget with a parent block containing child blocks that have the same structure.

Block List differs from the List in the following settings:
1. Extra styles
Number of list elements per line.
By default, there are 3 elements. The Auto parameter is available. You can also enter an arbitrary number.
Horizontal alignment
Even height of list elements.

2. Extra widgets
A Block List can be switched to one of the following widgets:
  • Slider
  • Ticker
Apply Independent Structure is the Block List’s setting similar to that of the List (Settings tab in the right panel).
Using it, you can make the structure of the list elements independent of each other. That is, each element of the list can have different elements and widgets with different styles. The list elements themselves will have the same style.
Once this setting is enabled, it cannot be cancelled.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_BUTTON:
Button serves as your call to action (CTA) and can contain links to pages, pop-up forms, and more.

The size of a Button is determined by the text inside (text length, font size, line height) and padding. You can also customize the Button’s size by changing its width and height in the Styles panel > Size section.

You can add an image (e.g., an arrow) inside the Button instead of text or together with it. To do this, you can set an image using Button’s Background settings. You can also add a Picture or an Icon inside the button itself.

Also, you can set styles: round the Button’s corners by changing the radius under the Size section, or add a shadow in Effects > Shadows.
Styles can be changed or disabled for different states of the Button (on hover, or on click).

You can configure an Action that will occur when hovering or clicking on a Button in the Logic tab in the right panel.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_CHECKBOX:
Checkbox is an element that can be used by the user to answer closed (Yes/No) questions. Best used when the user may or may not agree to some condition. Checkbox allows you to make single or multiple-choice selections.

Checkbox settings
Field name is a header transmitted to the data with the form submission.
List items is a list of options (questions that the user must answer). You can add, remove, rename, and swap options.
Required field, if set, prohibits the user from submitting a form without filling in this field.

By default, the Checkbox has custom styles, that is, you can set styles for all elements of the Checkbox. In the Form settings, you can enable ¨Apply standard browser styles to Checkbox and Radio button¨. In this case the Checkbox will have the browser styles (you will not be able to set styles for the Checkbox states).
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_COLLECTION:
Collection widget allows you to display a list of elements from a collection created in the site on the page. To do this:
1. Create a collection by clicking on the Collections icon on the left panel. Add the necessary fields to the table.
2. Populate the collection with data by adding elements to the table.
3. Add the Collection widget to the canvas and link it to the created collection.
4. Add interface elements to the Collection widget where the data from the collection will be displayed. For example, text elements, images, and others. Link the collection fields to the elements in the Settings on the right panel.

You can add any number of Collection widgets in different places on the site.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_COLUMNS:
Columns are designed to restore order among the elements within a row.
When you add the “Columns” widget, you get one parent row with two columns inside. Display settings: Flex horizontal are configured for the parent row by default, all Flex settings are available.
In the parent row there are two child blocks - columns, the width of which can be changed by dragging the Side face to the right or left. For them, too, the Display settings: Flex horizontal are configured by default, all Flex settings are available.
By manipulating the Flex parameters, you can adapt the content located in the columns.
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_CONTAINER:
Container is a horizontally centered element in the parent element. By default, it comes with a 960px max-width. If you need a Container of a different width, change it in the Style Settings.

Using Containers
A Container is used to keep the content centered on the page. It is recommended to put a Container inside a Section. It will be placed exactly in the center of a Section to conveniently put the content in it.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_DIV:
Div Block is the most basic and versatile element used in web site building. Sections, Containers, Buttons, are Div Blocks with certain extra properties. By default, the width of a Div Block is 100%, and the height automatically adjusts to fit the content inside the block.

Div Blocks can be used:
  • To create space between other blocks or as dividers
  • To group other elements together.
By adding multiple elements to a Div Block (for example, an image, text, or a button), you can manage them as an integral whole. This makes it possible to determine the position of child elements in a Div Block, align them, and set spaces between them and other elements. You can nest one Div within another.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_EMBED:
You can use Embed Widget to embed your own code to your web page.

Embed allows:
  • add additional functionality to a website
  • set extra styles
  • embed third-party services (for example, YouTube video)
The widget supports HTML code, CSS code with style tag and JavaScript code with script tag.

Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_FORM:
Form is an element for creating forms on the site for user input. Form has the width of its parent element and structurally contains 3 child elements that correspond to its 3 states:
  • Default state - contains all form elements
  • Success state - contains the message returned after successful form submission
  • Error State - contains the message returned after failed form submission
These states are switched in the Form settings in the Settings tab in the right panel. To customize a Form in different states, select the desired state in the Form state option, and then edit how a Form will be displayed in a selected state on the Canvas.

Adding elements in the Form
Default state Form contains the following elements:
  • Form title
  • Name input field
  • E-mail input field
  • Submit button
You can add or remove any element of the Form to customize its functionality. Also, if necessary, you can add basic elements to a Form, such as a Text element or a Div Block, as well as any other element or widget. You need to add elements to a block with all elements, i.e. a form in Default State.Form elements can only be dragged inside a Form

Structure of Form elements
Each Form element (except the Submit button) is a group of elements with the following structure:
  • Field Label – a text element with a field name that is displayed on a webpage. You can edit text of a Field Label and also remove a Label from the page.
  • Field (name depending on the element) – an element of a Form itself, with which the user interacts.
  • Error Message is a message that is displayed when there is an error in a field (for example, if a required field is not filled). The error message can be changed by switching to Error in the Fields state option in the Form Settings and then editing the message text on the Canvas.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_IMAGE:
Image element is used to add, edit, and style images.

An Image element is independent of other elements and distinct from a background image that is set on an element like a Section or a Div Block.

The following image file types can be used: SVG, JPG, PNG, GIF, WEBP. The max file size for an uploaded image is 64 MB.

A variety of styles can be set for the Image, including size, placement style, corner radius, shadows and filters, and SEO settings.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_INPUT:
Input field is used to collect one-line data.

Customizing Input field
Field name is a title that is transmitted to the data with the form submission results.
Type is a data type entered by the user in a field:
  • Plain text
  • E-mail
  • Phone
  • Number
  • Date
Phone data type has additional settings, such as the Field mask, which allows you to select a template for entering a phone number for different countries.
Required field, if set, prohibits the user from submitting a form without filling in this field.
Placeholder is a text that is displayed in an empty input field. It will be overwritten at the moment when the user starts typing his text in a field. This can be a field title or any default text.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_LINK_BLOCK:
Link Block can turn any element, like an image, or a banner, into a link.

Link Blocks are similar to Div Blocks in that they can be used for structure and layout, but the area inside of a Link Block becomes a link. They are often used to create layouts, like product or service lists that link to other pages.

Except for other links, any text block can be dragged into a Link block and design it as a Button.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_LIST:
List is a widget for arranging the content in blocks with the repeated structure.

List consists of a parent block with child elements within it, which, by default, have the same structure and styles.

You can add, remove, and reorder list items using List settings in the Settings tab in the right panel.

You can add any elements and widgets to the List Item, except for the Form.

Apply Independent Structure is the List’s setting (Settings tab in the right panel).

Using it, you can make the structure of the list elements independent of each other. That is, each element of the list can have different elements and widgets with different styles. The list elements themselves will have the same style. Once this setting is enabled, it cannot be cancelled.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_MAP:
Map is a widget to embed an interactive map into the page. You can use Google maps or Yandex maps. For the map to work correctly, you need to add an API key in the site settings in the Dashboard.

On the Settings tab, in the right panel, you can select the type of map service, as well as set the map display options: scale, language, center of coordinates, and points on the map.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_MENU:
Menu is a widget to arrange page navigation on a web site.
Settings tab in the right panel provides access to the following settings:
General Settings
They allow to define how the Menu will be used on mobile devices. By moving the slider, you set at what resolution a Hamburger Menu and a sidebar will be applied. By default, mobile Hamburger Menu is not applied at any resolution.
Menu Structure
By default, the Menu contains only items of the first level - Menu (1 lvl.). You can add items of the second level and beyond by clicking on "+" to the right of the heading.You can specify settings for menu items, including their location and type. By default, the Menu already has several first-level items. Menu items of any level can be renamed, moved, or deleted. You can also configure the actions to occur when clicking on each menu item.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_PAYMENT:
The Payment widget is used to create an order payment form on the website. To set up a payment form, first you need to connect at least one payment system in the site settings in the Dashboard.

For each product, you need to add an independent payment form and set up its parameters.

You can manage the payment form settings on the Settings tab:
Form name. Write the name that will be displayed in the list of orders in the site settings.
Form state. Set up the form design for default, success or error events. Using these settings, you can provide the user with information about the payment status, as well as instructions in case of problems with payment.
Fields state. Set up default fields and when data is entered incorrectly.
Payment. This allows connecting a payment system to the payment form. Select one of the payment services added in the project settings and specify the product name and its price. The payment form will not work without setting these parameters.

Data on orders made by users through the payment form will be displayed in the site settings in the Dashboard — Leads.

Note: If your site or team has expired, users will be unable to pay for orders with the payment form.

If there are problems with the payment form (for example, not all fields are filled in during setup, site or team has expired, the payment system has been deleted), a warning icon will be displayed on the layers panel to the right of the form name. In this case, users will not be able to make payments and the form error status will be displayed. Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_POPUP:
Pop-up is a widget for creating windows with important information, a feedback form, or other content, without closing which the visitor cannot interact with the site.

Pop-up will appear when clicking on a button or automatically. You can apply any styles, i.e. size, background color, etc., to it.

When added, Pop-up appears on the Canvas. It is the first element in the Layers list in the left panel.

You can display or hide a Pop-up using the Settings tab in the right panel.
A Pop-up can be closed by clicking on the overlay layer (the darkened layer below the Pop-up). It is also possible to enable the Close button on the overlay layer.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_RADIO:
Radio buttons allow the user to select one out of multiple options.

Radio buttons settings
Field name is a header transmitted to the data with the form submission.
List items is a list of options, from which the user must select the required one. You can add, remove, rename, and swap options.
Required field, if set, prohibits the user from submitting a form without filling in this field.

By default, the Radio buttons have custom styles.In the Form settings, you can enable ¨Apply standard browser styles to Checkbox and Radio button¨. In this case the Checkbox will have the browser styles (you will not be able to set styles for the Radio buttons states).
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_RICH_TEXT:
Rich Text allows you to create and format various types of text and media content — headings, lists, links, quotes, images, videos, and more.
This widget is ideal for large text blocks, such as blog articles, news posts, or product descriptions.
Connect it to a collection to display dynamic content. Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_SEARCH:
Search allows site visitors to find information they need.

You can customize the Search Bar type, as well as a Placeholder and its styles.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_SECTION:
Section is an element with a 100% width that can be expanded to fit the full width of the browser window. By default, a Section automatically adjusts to fit its content. Section height changes when adding elements to it.

Using Sections
Sections are used to create independent content blocks and to divide the page into relevant segments. They help creating a logical page structure.
Sections can be used, for example, to create a header, ‘About’ block, ‘Our Services’ block, or a footer. To get a consistent site structure, add Sections onto the Canvas and then arrange content within them.

Full-screen height Section
In some cases, it is necessary to have a Section fitting the whole screen or a part of it, for example, a site header. To do this, set the Section min height to 100vh (View Height) and it will fill 100% of the height of the browser window.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_SELECT:
Select field is a drop-down list that allows to select one (by default) or several list items.

Select field settings
Field name is a header transmitted to the data with the form submission.
List items is a list of options, from which the user must select the required one. You can add, remove, rename, and swap options.
Required field, if set, prohibits the user from submitting without filling in this field.
Allow multiple selection can be used to select multiple options at the same time.

By default, a Select field has custom styles. You can enable ¨Apply standard browser styles to Select¨ to be in the browser styles (in this case, it will be impossible to set styles for the Select field states).
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_SLIDER:
Slider is a dynamic page block that displays several content elements, adding interactivity to the website. For example, with the Slider you can publish a portfolio or customer feedback. Slides can be scrolled automatically or by clicking on the element.

The Slider is based on the element with the parent block, inside which you can place all the necessary elements and widgets.

By default, all slides have the same structure. This means that by adding the Image widget to the first slide, the image will be added to all other slides.
If you want to place different content on your slides, enable the «Apply Independent Structure» setting. This way you can add different widgets to each slide. For example, place text on the first slide and images on subsequent slides.

Settings
Move to the Settings tab in the right panel to customize the Slider display and their number. You can choose the effect and direction of slide scrolling, manage pagination. For example, select vertical scrolling so that the slides scroll from top to bottom, slow down its speed and enable automatic scrolling so that the site visitor has time to look through all the information placed on the slide.
Here you can also manage pagination, i.e. slide numbering. For example, you can replace standard dots with numbers or disable pagination.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_SVG_ICON:
The widget lets you add an icon in SVG format to your page.
Unlike a regular image, you can change the icon’s color directly in the widget. This makes it easy to quickly match icons to your site’s overall style. You can also adjust the icon’s size, auto-layout, positioning, and other properties.
When added to the canvas, the widget includes a default system icon. To replace it with your own, double-click the icon or go to Settings → Replace.
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_TABS:
Tabs is a widget to display content that is grouped in tabs, between which the user can switch. The widget includes:
  • Tabs Menu with Tabs Titles
  • Tabs Content with Content Panels, each of which belongs to one of the tabs
You can add any elements and widgets into a Tab Title and a Content Panel.

On the Settings tab, in the right panel, you can add, delete, and reorder Tabs’ elements. Settings are also used to switch between the tabs and also for their editing.

Horizontal and vertical Tabs
By default, the widget has a horizontal view. You can change it to vertical on the Styles tab in the right panel. To do this:
  1. For the Tabs element in the Auto Layout section, set the Direction to horizontal (right arrow),
  2. For the Tabs Menu element in the Auto Layout section, set the Direction to vertical (down arrow).
Tabs Titles elements have the same structure and styles by default. The same is also with the Content Panels. You can use the Apply independent structure** setting with the Tabs (on the Settings tab in the right panel). It allows you to make the structure of the elements independent of each other. That is, each element can have different elements and widgets with different styles. Once this setting is enabled, it cannot be cancelled.
Learn more
DE_HELP_ELEMENTS_TEXT:
Text is a versatile text element that is best used for non-link text.

In the Text settings (Settings tab in the right panel), you can select a tag for the text: heading H1, H2, and so on up to H6. The default Text tag is the div. Tag selection can be important for search engine optimization.

With the bottom boundary of the viewport you can change the start boundary of the animation. When the start point set for the trigger element reaches this boundary, the animation starts. The default value is 100%, i.e. the lower boundary of the visibility area. In the page preview mode, the setting is displayed as a green scroller-start marker.

For the viewport boundaries, you can set values using keywords (top, center, and bottom), or in pixels or percentages of the viewport height.

DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_AUTO_LAYOUT:
You can choose from the following display settings:
Flex
The Flex property allows flexible control over the arrangement of child elements within a container. It enables you to align child elements in one of two directions—row or column—and align them relative to each other horizontally and vertically. You can also define wrapping to the next line and adjust spacing between elements. Learn more

Block
Each element with the Block property starts on a new line and takes up the full width of its parent element. You can adjust the element's width in the Size settings. However, regardless of the set width or content, the element will push the following element to a new line, making it impossible to place such elements on the same line.

Grid
The Grid property allows you to create a grid consisting of rows and columns. It enables the creation of complex layouts and makes it easy to control the positioning of elements within the grid. Learn more

None
The None property allows you to completely hide an element, as if it were not on the canvas. All child elements will also be hidden. None can be used to show or hide elements depending on the display device or user actions.

Inline-block
All elements with the Inline-block property are placed side by side in a single line as long as they fit. If the line overflows, the elements start wrapping to the next line. The width of the elements is determined by their content.

Inline-Flex
The Inline-flex property is used to place multiple flex containers in a single line (similar to Inline-block elements). This cannot be achieved with the Flex property, as such an element takes up the full width of the line, regardless of its content.

1. Local Styles and Custom Classes
This feature allows you to manage an element's styles by configuring its local styles and custom classes. The interface displays the following:
  • Local Style Label: These styles apply exclusively to the specific element. The label is active (highlighted) by default when the element is added or when its styles are accessed.

An exclamation mark next to a class name indicates that the created class currently does not have any styles assigned to the selected breakpoint. The open class, whose styles you are editing, is highlighted in the color of the current breakpoint.
Within this interface, you can create new classes for an element, assign previously created classes to it, and switch between them. Once you select the desired class, define the styles you wish to save.
The values of specified properties are always highlighted in the color of the current breakpoint.
Display of Properties When Local Styles Are Open:
The value of a property defined in a custom class is hidden under a tag displaying the name of the class. Clicking on the tag switches to the class, allowing you to modify its styles.
Display of Properties When a Custom Class Is Open:
The value of a property defined in a higher-priority class (positioned further down the list) is hidden under a tag displaying the name of that class.

2. States
You can set custom styles for different states: on hover, active, in focus. For example, you can change the color of a button on hover. To do this, click "+" next to the list of states and select the desired value.
To set a custom style, you can select the desired state and override its styles in the Design tab. If you want to add a state to an element to which a custom class has been assigned, open class settings in the Applied Classes menu and select the desired one.

DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_EFFECTS_BACKDROP_FI:
This setting allows you to apply visual effects to the background or the background image behind an element. For the effect to be visible, the element or its background must be fully or partially transparent.

You can choose from the following filters:
  • Blur
  • Drop Shadow

DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_EFFECTS_BLEND_MODE:
The setting allows you to blend the color of the element with the layers beneath it. Using blend modes, you can create various effects:
  • Normal – The default setting. Colors do not blend.
  • Multiply – The colors of the elements are multiplied. The effect is like applying images onto a transparent film.
  • Screen – The colors of the elements are multiplied and then the result is inverted. The effect is like the glowing images on a projection screen.
  • Overlay – Multiplies or lightens the color of the element depending on the color of the layer below. If the lower color is darker, the result is like in Multiply effect; if the lower color is lighter, the result is like in Screen effect.
  • Darken – The color of the bottom element is replaced with the color of the top element in areas where the top element is darker.
  • Lighten – The color of the bottom element is replaced with the color of the top element in areas where the top element is lighter.
  • Color-dodge – The color of the bottom element is lightened to reflect the color of the top element.
  • Color-burn – The color of the bottom element is darkened to reflect the color of the top element.
  • Hard-light – Multiplies or lightens the color of the element depending on the color of the bottom element. If the bottom color is lighter, the result is like in Multiply effect; if the bottom color is darker, the result is like in Screen effect. The effect is like the glow of a strong spotlight in the background.
  • Soft-light – The mode is similar to Hard-light effect, but the result is softer. The effect is like the glow of a diffused spotlight in the background.
  • Difference – Subtracts the darker color from the lighter color.
  • Exclusion – Similar to Difference effect, but with less contrast.
  • Hue – Creates a color that has the hue of the top color, but the saturation and brightness of the bottom color.
  • Saturation – Creates a color that has the saturation of the top color, but the hue and brightness of the bottom color.
  • Color – Creates a color that has the hue and saturation of the top color, but the brightness of the bottom color.
  • Luminosity – Creates a color that has the brightness of the top color, but the hue and saturation of the bottom color. The result is the opposite of Color effect.
Learn more

Filters apply a unique visual effect to an element. Filters can also be used to create changes on hover states. You can add filters on an image or any other element. Filters change the appearance of the entire element, including its children.

There are the following filters:

You can also add multiple filters to a single element and toggle their visibility and order in the filter settings.

DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_EFFECTS_TRANSFORMS:
Transforms
You can add transforms to manipulate an element's appearance and position on different states, such as hover.
There are 4 types of transforms:
  • Move
  • Scale
  • Rotate
  • Skew
You can add more than one type of transform without losing any of your previous settings. Use the plus icon to add an additional transform.
Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_EFFECTS_TRANSITIONS:
Transitions
Transitions create a smooth animation between different states of an element. A duration and easing type effect can be customized to create a unique transition. A common use of transitions is for hover states on elements so that they don’t abruptly change on hover.

Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_GAP: In the settings, you can set the space value between rows or columns in Auto Layout. For this to work, assign Flex or Inline-flex to the Display option in Auto Layout.
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_MIN_MAX_SIZE:
Min and max dimensions
Min and Max width and height of an element allow you to adjust its dimensions when the viewport changes. The element will not shrink below the minimum width (height) or grow above the maximum.
This can be used, for example, to create a Section with a full screen height. By setting the minimum section dimensions to 100vh, its height will fill 100% of the screen height even with little content, and in the case of content growth it will become larger and the content will not be cropped.

Overflow
Overflow is used to control the content that goes beyond the borders of an element when it overflows with content.
Overflow occurs automatically when an element has a fixed width and height and there is not enough space for the content inside.

By default, text that does not fit the element is visible (Visible icon is selected).

Other overflow options:

Hidden
Content that extends beyond the borders of an element is hidden.
 Scroll
A scroll bar (depending on OS and browser) is displayed to allow users to scroll the content.
* Auto
Allows users to scroll content if it overflows an element.

Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_POSITION:
Position
The position property determines an element’s position on the page.
It allows you to change the flow of elements on the page by shifting them or fixing their position. Once you set an element's position, you can adjust the top, bottom, left, and right properties.

There are the following types of positioning:
  • Static – element's standard position, no offset.
  • Relative – an element can be moved relative to its original position by specifying the coordinates.
  • Absolute – an element can be moved relative to its parent element by specifying the coordinates.
  • Fixed – you can fix the position of an element, which will not change when scrolling the page.
  • Sticky – depending on the scroll position of the browser window, an element will have fixed or relative position.
Position settings also allows setting the Z-index, which determines the position of an element on an imaginary z-axis, passing in and out of the screen of your computer.

Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_SIZE:
In the Size settings, you can set the width and height of an element.

The following parameters are available for setting sizes: automatic size (auto), pixels (px), percentages (%), em, Viewport Width (vw), Viewport Height (vh).

In the Size settings, you can also set the following properties:
  • Min or Max. Minimum and maximum element height and width.
  • Overflow. Controls the content that overflows the borders of an element when content overflows.
  • Opacity. Determines the opacity of an element and its children.
  • Round all corners, Individual radius. Sets the value of the rounding radius for all corners of an element, or an individual radius for each corner.
Margins and Paddings

Margins
Define the space between the borders of an element and its adjacent elements. Highlighted in yellow.

Paddings
Define the space between the borders of an element and its content. Highlighted in green.

You can set Margins and Paddings both on all sides and selectively. Margins and paddings size can be set in px, %, and em.

To set indents, move the mouse cursor over the corresponding indent, hold down the left mouse button and drag the arrow that appears in the desired direction.

For simultaneous changing margins and paddings:
  • from two opposite sides of an element – hold down the Alt key while dragging the arrow with the mouse
  • from four sides of an element – hold down the Shift key while dragging the arrow with the mouse

Center an element horizontally with Margins
Margins can have not only a fixed value, but also an auto value. The Auto setting for both left and right margins is used to horizontally center an element relative to its parent.

Negative Margin
Margins can take negative values. In this case, an element is shifted to the margin side and overlaps the adjacent elements.
Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_SPACING:
To set the spacing between elements, you need to set margins and paddings.

Margins define the distance between the borders of an element and its neighboring elements. Paddings define the space between the borders of an element and its content.

By default, the spacing settings are collapsed, so you can set opposite margins or paddings simultaneously.

Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_TEXT:
To format Text, the following options are used:
Font
To set a font, open the list and select the desired one (Google fonts are used).
Font weight
Depending on the selected font, this list will contain options for its weight and style (italic).
Font size
Font size can be set in the following units:
  • Pixels (px)
    The font size is set in pixels (base unit).
  • Em
    The font size is relative to the parent element’s font size. 1 em is equal to the element’s current font size. The value of the property can be less than 1, then the element's font size will be less than the parent one, or more than 1, then the element’s font size will be larger than the parent one.
    Example: Parent element’s font size is 16px. By setting the child element’s font size to 0.5em, we get the element’s font size equal to 8px. The child element’s font size will change in proportion to changes in the parent element’s font size.
  • Percentage (%)
    The size is defined in percentage of the parent element’s font size. The child element’s font size will change in proportion to changes in the parent element’s font size.
  • Viewport Width (vw)
    A percentage of the total width of the browser window. 10vw represents 10% of the browser window’s current width (e.g. 48px on a mobile phone with a 480px screen).
  • Viewport Height (vh)
    A percentage of the total height of the browser window. 10vh is 10% of the browser window’s current height.
Font color
You can set the text color by entering a color value (in Hex or RGB format) or by picking a color with the color picker. You can also adjust the opacity of the text color.
Line-height
The spacing between lines of text is set to Normal by default and depends on the browser. You can adjust the spacing between lines by selecting a value in px, em, %, vw, vh, or num (a numeric value depending on the browser’s font size, typically 16px = 1 num).
Letter-spacing
Letter spacing is set to Normal by default and depends on the selected font. You can change the letter spacing by selecting a value in px, em, vw, or vh.
Text Align
You can align text:
  • by width by choosing alignment to the left, center or right
  • by height by selecting alignment on the top edge, in the middle or on the bottom edge.
Learn more
DE_HELP_RIGHT_STYLES_TEXT_OPTION:
More Type Options:
Decoration
Text can be selected with underlining or strikethrough.
Capitalize
All letters of the text can be converted to upper case, lower case, or each word can be capitalized.
Overflow wrap
Overflow wrap specifies whether or not lines with long words can be broken, if they overflow the container. The function is enabled by default.
Breaking / White-space
Breaking property controls how white-space is handled inside an element:
  • Normal - Text in the browser window is displayed as usual, line breaks are set automatically. This is a default value.  
  • Nowrap - Spaces are ignored, all text is displayed on one line.Pre - Text is shown with all spaces and breaks as they were added. If the line is too long to fit in the browser window, a horizontal scroll bar will be added.
  • Pre-wrap - All spaces and breaks are preserved in the text but if the width of the line does not fit in the specified area, the text will be automatically wrapped to the next line.
  • Pre-line - Spaces are ignored in the text, the text is automatically wrapped to the next line if it does not fit in the specified area.
  • Break-spaces – The same as pre-wrap, except that any sequence of permanent spaces always takes space, including at the end of a line. The line can be broken after each permanent space, including between spaces.
Learn more
